channel.title: CyberNetSec - Threat Intelligence
channel.description: Latest threat intelligence threat intelligence - the last 10 articles

item.title: QR Code Phishing Surges 146% in Q1 2026, Microsoft Warns
item.description:
According to Microsoft's Q1 2026 threat intelligence report, QR code-based phishing (quishing) has become the fastest-growing email attack vector, with volumes jumping 146% between January and March. Attackers are increasingly hiding malicious QR codes within PDF attachments to bypass email security...

Severity: High

Categories: Phishing, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: UK Cyber Survey: 43% of Businesses Breached, But Only 25% Have an IR Plan
item.description:
The UK government's latest Cyber Security Breaches Survey for 2025/2026 reveals a landscape of persistent risk, with 43% of businesses (an estimated 612,000) experiencing a breach in the past year. Phishing remains the dominant attack vector, implicated in 85% of successful breaches. Despite the hig...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: That AI Extension Helping You Write? It's Actually a RAT Stealing Your Data
item.description:
Palo Alto Networks' Unit 42 has discovered at least 18 malicious browser extensions masquerading as Generative AI productivity tools. These extensions, some downloaded by thousands, are not enhancing productivity but are instead functioning as Remote Access Trojans (RATs), infostealers, and search h...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Threat Actor

Read Full Article →

item.title: Critical 'Copy Fail' Linux Flaw (CVE-2026-31431) Gives Instant Root on Major Distros
item.description:
A critical logic flaw in the Linux kernel, dubbed 'Copy Fail' and tracked as CVE-2026-31431, has been disclosed, affecting nearly all major distributions since 2017. Discovered by Theori, the vulnerability allows any unprivileged local user to gain full root privileges with a simple, highly reliable...

Severity: Critical

Categories: Vulnerability, Threat Intelligence, Patch Management

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol IOCTA Report: AI, Encryption, and Data Theft are Fueling an Industrialized Cybercrime Wave
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) report warns of an industrialized cybercrime landscape where AI, encryption, and proxies are making attacks faster and more sophisticated. The report highlights a significant shift in ransomware tactics towards data theft and extortio...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Threat Intelligence, Policy and Compliance, Ransomware

Read Full Article →

item.title: New Stealthy Python Backdoor 'DEEP#DOOR' Steals Credentials Using Legitimate Tunneling Service
item.description:
Security researchers from Securonix have discovered a sophisticated, multi-stage Python backdoor named DEEP#DOOR. The malware operates as a full-featured Remote Access Trojan (RAT), beginning with an obfuscated batch script that embeds the Python payload. It disables Windows security controls, estab...

Severity: High

Categories: Malware, Threat Intelligence, Cyberattack

Read Full Article →

item.title: Europol Warns of AI-Powered 'Industrialized Cybercrime' in IOCTA 2026 Report
item.description:
Europol's 2026 Internet Organised Crime Threat Assessment (IOCTA) highlights a major shift towards the 'industrialization' of cybercrime, significantly accelerated by the use of Artificial Intelligence. The report states that AI is being used to increase the scale, speed, and efficiency of attacks. ...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: Singapore Plans Major Cybersecurity Overhaul, Mandating PQC and Expanding CII Oversight
item.description:
Singapore is set to significantly strengthen its national cybersecurity posture with a series of regulatory updates announced by the government. The changes, to be rolled out over the next two years, will expand cybersecurity obligations to systems connected to Critical Information Infrastructure (C...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →

item.title: AI-Driven Attacks Fueling MSP Supply Chain Risk, Guardz Report Finds
item.description:
A new report from cybersecurity firm Guardz reveals a grim reality for SMBs, with nine out of ten having compromised users, largely due to AI-accelerated attacks. The 2026 State of MSP Threat Report highlights a massive surge in losses from Business Email Compromise (BEC) and identifies the abuse of...

Severity: High

Categories: Supply Chain Attack, Threat Intelligence, Malware

Read Full Article →

item.title: State CISO Confidence Plummets as AI Threats Rise and Budgets Fall, Survey Finds
item.description:
Confidence among U.S. state chief information security officers (CISOs) has dropped dramatically, according to the 2026 NASCIO-Deloitte Cybersecurity Study. Only 26% of state CISOs feel 'extremely' or 'very' confident in their ability to protect state systems, a steep decline from 48% in 2022. The p...

Severity: Informational

Categories: Policy and Compliance, Regulatory, Threat Intelligence

Read Full Article →
